data.text: I’m checking the frontend routes, the existing artifact detail page, and the ddx bead contract so I can confirm scope before editing anything. After that I’ll map the acceptance criteria to concrete files and tests, then implement only the needed slice.

data.text: I found existing artifact type panel code already wired into the detail route, so this is likely a completion/verification pass rather than a greenfield implementation. I’m reading the panel, its helper module, and the artifact page/tests now to see which acceptance criteria are already satisfied and which gaps remain.

data.output:
{#if selectedTypeDefinition}

{#if collision} {/if}

{#if activeTab === 'referencePrompt'}
Reference Prompt

{selectedTypeDefinition.prompt.content}

Template

{#if selectedTypeDefinition.examples.length === 0}

No examples provided.

{:else} {#each selectedTypeDefinition.examples as example}
{example.path} {#if example.description} · {example.description} {/if} {#if example.isTruncated} Truncated {/if} 
{example.content}
{/each} {/if}
{/if}
{/if} ==== export interface ArtifactTypeDefinitionFile { path: string; content: string; isTruncated: boolean; sizeBytes: number; } export interface ArtifactTypeDefinitionExample { path: string; description?: string | null; content: string; isTruncated: boolean; sizeBytes: number; } export interface ArtifactTypeDefinition { plugin: string; typeId: string; name: string; description: string; prefix: string; pattern: string; phase: string; sourceMetaPath: string; template: ArtifactTypeDefinitionFile; prompt: ArtifactTypeDefinitionFile; examples: ArtifactTypeDefinitionExample[]; } export type ArtifactTypeTab = 'referencePrompt' | 'template' | 'examples'; export const ARTIFACT_TYPE_TABS: { value: ArtifactTypeTab; label: string }[] = [ { value: 'referencePrompt', label: 'Reference Prompt' }, { value: 'template', label: 'Template' }, { value: 'examples', label: 'Examples' } ]; export function artifactTypeKey(def: ArtifactTypeDefinition): string { return `${def.plugin}::${def.typeId}::${def.sourceMetaPath}`; } export function artifactTypeLabel(def: ArtifactTypeDefinition): string { return def.name || `${def.plugin}/${def.typeId}`; } export function hasArtifactTypeCollision(definitions: ArtifactTypeDefinition[]): boolean { return definitions.length > 1; } export function selectedArtifactTypeDefinition( definitions: ArtifactTypeDefinition[], typeDefParam: string | null ): ArtifactTypeDefinition | null { if (definitions.length === 0) return null; if (!typeDefParam) return definitions[0]; return definitions.find((def) => artifactTypeKey(def) === typeDefParam) ?? definitions[0]; } export function updateTypeDefUrl(url: URL, typeDefParam: string | null): string { const next = new URL(url); if (typeDefParam) next.searchParams.set('typeDef', typeDefParam); else next.searchParams.delete('typeDef'); return next.pathname + next.search; } ==== import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'; import { artifactTypeKey, hasArtifactTypeCollision, selectedArtifactTypeDefinition, updateTypeDefUrl, type ArtifactTypeDefinition } from './artifactTypePanel'; function def(overrides: Partial): ArtifactTypeDefinition { return { plugin: 'ddx', typeId: 'docs-sample', name: 'Docs sample', description: 'Example type', prefix: 'docs', pattern: 'docs/*.md', phase: 'frame', sourceMetaPath: 'plugins/ddx/types/docs-sample/meta.yml', template: { path: 'template.md', content: '# template', isTruncated: false, sizeBytes: 10 }, prompt: { path: 'prompt.md', content: '# prompt', isTruncated: false, sizeBytes: 8 }, examples: [], ...overrides }; } describe('artifactTypePanel helpers', () => { it('selects the single matching type definition by default', () => { const defs = [def({ typeId: 'docs-sample' })]; expect(selectedArtifactTypeDefinition(defs, null)).toBe(defs[0]); expect(hasArtifactTypeCollision(defs)).toBe(false); }); it('exposes collision state and stable keys for multiple matches', () => { const defs = [def({ plugin: 'alpha' }), def({ plugin: 'beta' })]; expect(hasArtifactTypeCollision(defs)).toBe(true); expect(artifactTypeKey(defs[0])).toBe('alpha::docs-sample::plugins/ddx/types/docs-sample/meta.yml'); expect(artifactTypeKey(defs[1])).toBe('beta::docs-sample::plugins/ddx/types/docs-sample/meta.yml'); }); it('round-trips typeDef query state without dropping unrelated params', () => { const url = new URL('https://example.test/nodes/n/projects/p/artifacts/a?back=%2Flist&view=full'); expect(updateTypeDefUrl(url, 'alpha::docs-sample::plugins/ddx/types/docs-sample/meta.yml')).toBe( '/nodes/n/projects/p/artifacts/a?back=%2Flist&view=full&typeDef=alpha%3A%3Adocs-sample%3A%3Aplugins%2Fddx%2Ftypes%2Fdocs-sample%2Fmeta.yml' ); expect(updateTypeDefUrl(url, null)).toBe('/nodes/n/projects/p/artifacts/a?back=%2Flist&view=full'); }); });

data.text: The panel and helper module already exist, but the current tests are helper-only. I’m verifying whether the detail page actually passes the matching resolver data through end-to-end and whether component-level coverage is missing or broken.

{ const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch) const data = await client.request(ARTIFACT_DETAIL_QUERY, { projectID: params.projectId, id: decodeURIComponent(params.artifactId) }) // Build content URL for binary types (images, PDFs, unknown). // The UI uses this URL for , , and download links. const contentUrl = data.artifact ? `/api/projects/${encodeURIComponent(params.projectId)}/artifact-content?path=${encodeURIComponent(data.artifact.path)}` : null // Preserve back-link state so the detail page can return to the filtered list. const back = url.searchParams.get('back') ?? null // Probe whether the producing run exists, so the UI can disable the link // (with tooltip) when the run id is unknown. let runExists = false const gb = data.artifact?.generatedBy if (gb?.runId) { try { const probe = await client.request<{ run: { id: string } | null }>(RUN_EXISTS_QUERY, { id: gb.runId }) runExists = probe.run != null } catch { runExists = false } } return { nodeId: params.nodeId, projectId: params.projectId, artifactId: params.artifactId, artifact: data.artifact, contentUrl, back, runExists } } ==== import type { PageLoad } from './$types'; import { createClient } from '$lib/gql/client'; import { gql } from 'graphql-request'; import { readState } from '$lib/urlState'; import type { GroupBy } from './grouping'; export const ARTIFACTS_QUERY = gql` query Artifacts( $projectID: ID! $first: Int $after: String $mediaType: String $search: String $sort: ArtifactSort $staleness: String $phase: String $prefix: [String!] ) { artifacts( projectID: $projectID first: $first after: $after mediaType: $mediaType search: $search sort: $sort staleness: $staleness phase: $phase prefix: $prefix ) { edges { node { id path title mediaType staleness } cursor snippet } pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } totalCount } } `; export interface ArtifactNode { id: string; path: string; title: string; mediaType: string; staleness: string; } export interface ArtifactEdge { node: ArtifactNode; cursor: string; snippet?: string | null; } export interface PageInfo { hasNextPage: boolean; endCursor: string | null; } export interface ArtifactConnection { edges: ArtifactEdge[]; pageInfo: PageInfo; totalCount: number; } interface ArtifactsResult { artifacts: ArtifactConnection; } export const PAGE_SIZE = 50; export const SORT_OPTIONS = [ { value: 'PATH', label: 'Path' }, { value: 'TITLE', label: 'Title' }, { value: 'MODIFIED', label: 'Last modified' }, { value: 'DEPS_COUNT', label: 'Dependency count' }, { value: 'ID', label: 'ID' } ] as const; export type ArtifactSort = (typeof SORT_OPTIONS)[number]['value']; export const DEFAULT_SORT: ArtifactSort = 'PATH'; const SORT_VALUES = new Set(SORT_OPTIONS.map((o) => o.value)); export function parseSort(raw: string | null): ArtifactSort { return raw && SORT_VALUES.has(raw as ArtifactSort) ? (raw as ArtifactSort) : DEFAULT_SORT; } export const STALENESS_OPTIONS = ['fresh', 'stale', 'missing'] as const; export type Staleness = (typeof STALENESS_OPTIONS)[number]; const STALENESS_SET = new Set(STALENESS_OPTIONS); export function parseStaleness(raw: string | null): Staleness | null { return raw && STALENESS_SET.has(raw) ? (raw as Staleness) : null; } export const load: PageLoad = async ({ params, url, fetch }) => { const state = readState(url.searchParams); const hasGroupByParam = url.searchParams.has('groupBy'); const mediaType = state.mediaType; const q = state.q; const groupBy: GroupBy = state.groupBy; const sort = parseSort(state.sort); const staleness = parseStaleness(state.staleness); const phase = state.phase; const prefix = state.prefix; const client = createClient(fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch); const data = await client.request(ARTIFACTS_QUERY, { projectID: params.projectId, first: PAGE_SIZE, mediaType: mediaType ?? undefined, search: q ? q : undefined, sort, staleness: staleness ?? undefined, phase: phase ?? undefined, prefix: prefix.length > 0 ? prefix : undefined }); return { nodeId: params.nodeId, projectId: params.projectId, artifacts: data.artifacts, mediaType, q, groupBy, sort, staleness, phase, prefix, hasGroupByParam }; };

data.text: I’ve confirmed the detail route already renders the panel when `artifact.typeDefinitions.length > 0`, so the likely gap is test coverage, not the page wiring. Next I’m checking how this repo tests Svelte components so I can add the smallest reliable Vitest coverage instead of guessing at a harness.

The implementation surface looks mostly complete already, so I’m running the frontend unit gate now to see whether the remaining work is a true code gap or just a test gap. If the gate is green, I’ll only add what’s necessary to satisfy the bead’s verification contract.

data.text: The unit gate is blocked by missing local dependencies, not by a test failure. I’m checking whether this workspace already has the frontend install artifact; if not, I’ll hydrate dependencies and rerun the exact AC gates before touching code.

data.text: Dependencies are installing now; Playwright is pulling its browser bundle as part of the package install. Once that settles, I’ll rerun the exact frontend tests and then inspect whether any code changes are actually needed.

The existing unit suite has a failure unrelated to this bead’s panel work. I’m waiting for the full report so I can tell whether this is a pre-existing break or something introduced by the dependency install, then I’ll isolate the minimal fix path.

The blocker is now clear: the frontend unit suite fails in an unrelated `D3Graph` contrast spec, and the bead contract says not to widen scope to unrelated files. I’m collecting exact line references so the no-changes rationale is precise and auditable, then I’ll write that evidence file and stop.

I have enough evidence for the blocker, but I’m also checking the repo’s pre-commit hook once so the rationale can name the exact gate that stops the bead. If it fails on the same unrelated suite, that confirms this is an environment/scope issue rather than a panel implementation issue.

data.text: I’m writing the required no-changes evidence now. It will record the exact blocker, the files that already satisfy the bead’s frontend contract, and why I’m not widening scope into the unrelated failing spec.

Blocked by an unrelated frontend unit failure, so I wrote the required evidence file at [no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4fd71cf3-20260507T002126-0572d724/.ddx/executions/20260507T002126-0572d724/no_changes_rationale.txt).

`bun run test:unit -- --run` fails in [D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4fd71cf3-20260507T002126-0572d724/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/D3Graph.contrast.spec.ts:65) on the `stroke-opacity` assertion, which is outside this bead’s scope. The artifact-type panel work itself is already present and wired into the detail page in [ArtifactTypePanel.svelte](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4fd71cf3-20260507T002126-0572d724/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/ArtifactTypePanel.svelte) and [artifact detail page](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-4fd71cf3-20260507T002126-0572d724/cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/artifacts/[artifactId]/+page.svelte:17).
